text: Risk management is an essential skill in both business and everyday life. It begins with identifying potential risks, such as financial losses or delays in a project. Once risks are known, you can develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact. For example, you might buy insurance or improve safety procedures. However, not all risks can be avoided, so it is also important to have contingency plans. These are backup actions that you can take if something goes wrong. Finally, effective risk management requires continuous monitoring. By regularly checking for new risks and evaluating your strategies, you can respond quickly and keep projects on track.
